refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Validate that all safety plan requirements were met during trial.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to direct task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and enforce safety constraints during task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall measure and report productivity metrics (volume, tonnage, time) in real-time and post-operation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-performance vehicle with autonomous task planning and execution, coupled with real-time telemetry and safety monitoring to achieve target productivity within safety constraints.

DP-B: Provide dual-mode operation (on-site manual + remote teleoperation) with adaptive task sequencing, continuous safety validation, and productivity tracking to meet KPI under variable worksite conditions.

DP-C: Decompose productivity into vehicle capability (cycle time, accuracy), operator effectiveness (situational awareness, control latency), and safety compliance (constraint enforcement), each optimized independently then integrated.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Provide dual-mode operation (on-site manual + remote teleoperation) with adaptive task sequencing, continuous safety validation, and productivity tracking to meet KPI under variable worksite conditions.

potential_sources: Teleoperation latency may couple with vehicle control responsiveness; mitigated by network link SLA and vehicle control loop design.
Safety constraint enforcement couples with task planning; mitigated by explicit safety services module that gates all task commands.
Productivity measurement couples with telemetry fidelity; mitigated by redundant sensor logging and post-operation data validation.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B acknowledges that productivity is not solely a vehicle property but emerges from operator effectiveness + vehicle capability + safety compliance. Dual-mode operation (on-site vs. remote) is a key design choice that addresses stakeholder needs (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator) and allows graceful degradation if network link fails.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite network link (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) is available with <200ms latency for remote teleoperation; Risk: If latency exceeds threshold, remote mode becomes unsafe or ineffective.

Assumption: Vehicle control loop can achieve repeatable cycle time within ±5% of baseline; Risk: Wear, environmental variation, or operator skill variance may degrade cycle time over evaluation period.

Assumption: Safety Services module can enforce all worksite safety plan constraints without blocking legitimate task execution; Risk: Over-conservative safety logic may reduce achievable productivity below target.
Assumption: Productivity target is achievable with current vehicle hardware and operator skill level; Risk: Target may be unrealistic, requiring hardware upgrade or extended operator training.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify productivity_target value and units (m³/h or ton/h) early in decomposition. Define network link SLA (latency, availability, bandwidth) and vehicle control loop performance specs (cycle time repeatability, accuracy) as explicit DPs to enable independent verification.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to deliver dual-mode operation (on-site manual + remote teleoperation) with continuous productivity tracking and safety compliance. Owns the overall task execution workflow, mode selection logic, and KPI measurement.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands from Site Manager or Tele-operator (e.g., 'dig at location X', 'load truck Y'). NGHE Solution parses, validates against safety constraints, and routes to Vehicle or Teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Streams real-time vehicle state (position, orientation, bucket load, cycle status), operator state (mode, control inputs), and productivity metrics (volume moved, tonnage, elapsed time) to Site Manager and Tele-operator dashboards.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Broadcasts safety alerts (e.g., 'geofence violation', 'operator override', 'emergency stop') to Safety Manager and on-site personnel. Triggers automatic task abort if critical safety threshold breached.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel (Tasking in, Telemetry + SafetyAlert out) connecting on-site NGHE Solution to remote Tele-operator and Safety Manager. Latency SLA: <200ms for safe teleoperation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for DP-B (dual-mode operation with productivity tracking and safety validation). All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are logically contained. Minimal diffusion: only Network Link interface crosses boundary to external stakeholders.

Vehicle module owns task execution (dig, load, transport, dump) but does not own operator interface, safety validation, or productivity measurement. Assigning DP-B to Vehicle alone would scatter teleoperation logic, safety services, and KPI measurement across multiple modules, violating cohesion.

Teleoperation module owns remote operator interface and control latency but does not own on-site manual mode, vehicle task execution, safety constraint enforcement, or productivity measurement. Assigning DP-B to Teleoperation alone would omit on-site operation and safety services.

Safety Services module owns constraint validation and alert generation but does not own task execution, operator interface, or productivity measurement. Assigning DP-B to Safety Services alone would scatter vehicle control, teleoperation, and KPI measurement across other modules.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP-B (dual-mode operation with adaptive task sequencing, continuous safety validation, and productivity tracking) is best realized as an integrated orchestration layer within NGHE Solution. This module owns the task execution workflow state machine (on-site vs. remote mode selection), safety gate logic (before task dispatch), and KPI aggregation (real-time + post-operation). Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are subordinate modules that NGHE Solution coordinates via well-defined interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert).
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if task execution workflow logic is not cleanly separated from Vehicle control logic. Mitigation: Define explicit state machine (Idle → TaskReceived → SafetyValidated → Executing → Complete) and allocate each state transition to a specific module (NGHE Solution owns transitions; Vehicle owns execution within Executing state).

Risk: Productivity measurement logic may be scattered across Vehicle (cycle time), Teleoperation (operator efficiency), and Safety Services (constraint violations). Mitigation: Centralize KPI aggregation in NGHE Solution; Vehicle and Teleoperation report raw metrics (cycle time, control inputs) via Telemetry interface; NGHE Solution computes productivity = volume / elapsed_time.

Risk: Safety validation may be duplicated in NGHE Solution (task gating) and Safety Services (constraint checking). Mitigation: NGHE Solution calls Safety Services API to validate task before dispatch; Safety Services owns all constraint logic; NGHE Solution owns task workflow.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define explicit interface contracts between NGHE Solution and subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Specify: (1) Task command format (Tasking in), (2) Telemetry schema (vehicle state, operator state, KPI), (3) SafetyAlert schema (alert type, severity, action), (4) Network Link SLA (latency, availability). This will clarify module boundaries and enable independent testing.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-NGHE-001 (enable operator to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity ≥ target while maintaining safety compliance) is directly addressed by DP-B (dual-mode operation with adaptive task sequencing, continuous safety validation, and productivity tracking). DP-B is neither over-constrained nor under-specified; it provides a clear solution concept that can be decomposed into sub-DPs (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety services) at the next level.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Task execution workflow logic (state machine) may leak into Vehicle module if Vehicle is given direct access to Tasking interface. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns Tasking interface; Vehicle receives task commands only via internal API from NGHE Solution.

Diffusion Risk 2: Productivity measurement may be scattered if Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services each compute their own KPIs. Mitigation: Centralize KPI aggregation in NGHE Solution; subordinate modules report raw metrics only.

Diffusion Risk 3: Safety validation may be duplicated if NGHE Solution and Safety Services both check constraints. Mitigation: NGHE Solution calls Safety Services API; Safety Services owns all constraint logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for DP-B because it owns the orchestration layer (task workflow, mode selection, KPI aggregation) while delegating execution to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. This design respects the principle of cohesion (all productivity-related logic is in one module) and avoids diffusion (no duplication across modules).

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 (productivity ≥ target) is feasible via representative operations trial. NGHE Solution module owns Telemetry interface, which streams real-time KPI data (volume, tonnage, elapsed time) to Site Manager dashboard. Post-operation, KPI data is logged and compared against productivity_target. Safety compliance is verified by checking SafetyAlert logs for any constraint violations during trial.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution IM is clear: NGHE Solution owns the task execution workflow (Tasking in → SafetyValidated → Executing → Complete), which directly enables productivity measurement. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are allocated sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1, FR-NGHE-001.2, FR-NGHE-001.3) at the next decomposition level.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Define productivity_target value and units (m³/h or ton/h) explicitly in FR-NGHE-001. This is critical for verification and acceptance testing.
Revision 2: Define Network Link SLA (latency <200ms, availability >99.5%) as an explicit constraint on DP-B. This will inform Teleoperation module design and risk mitigation.

Revision 3: Define safety plan constraints (geofence, speed limits, operator override rules) as explicit inputs to Safety Services module. This will enable independent verification of safety compliance.
Revision 4: Clarify evaluation period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week, 4-week trial) in FR-NGHE-001. This will inform KPI aggregation logic in NGHE Solution.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that has been successfully refined into atomic sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4), each addressing a distinct concern (vehicle task execution, operator interface, safety compliance, productivity measurement). DP-B (dual-mode operation with adaptive task sequencing, continuous safety validation, and productivity tracking) is a coherent solution concept that maps cleanly to NGHE Solution IM. All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. The next step is to decompose each sub-FR into its own FR-DP-IM cycle, starting with FR-NGHE-001.1 (vehicle task execution) and FR-NGHE-001.3 (safety compliance), which are safety-critical and should be designed first.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to direct task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and enforce safety constraints during task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall measure and report productivity metrics (volume, tonnage, time) in real-time and post-operation.

assumptions: Assumption A1: Worksite network link (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) is available with <200ms latency for remote teleoperation.
Assumption A2: Vehicle control loop can achieve repeatable cycle time within ±5% of baseline.
Assumption A3: Safety Services module can enforce all worksite safety plan constraints without blocking legitimate task execution.
Assumption A4: Productivity target is achievable with current vehicle hardware and operator skill level.
Assumption A5: Site Manager and Tele-operator can interpret Telemetry and SafetyAlert streams in real-time to make task decisions.
Assumption A6: Evaluation period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week) is representative of normal worksite operations.
open_risks: Risk R1: Network latency exceeds 200ms, making remote teleoperation unsafe or ineffective. Mitigation: Define Network Link SLA early; conduct latency testing before trial.

Risk R2: Vehicle cycle time degrades over evaluation period due to wear or environmental variation. Mitigation: Establish baseline cycle time; monitor for drift; adjust productivity_target if necessary.

Risk R3: Safety Services logic is over-conservative, blocking legitimate task execution and reducing achievable productivity below target. Mitigation: Validate safety constraints with Safety Manager before trial; allow operator override with audit trail.
Risk R4: Productivity target is unrealistic, requiring hardware upgrade or extended operator training. Mitigation: Conduct feasibility study before trial; compare target against industry benchmarks.
Risk R5: Productivity measurement is inaccurate due to sensor drift or telemetry loss. Mitigation: Implement redundant sensors; validate telemetry against manual measurement during trial.

Risk R6: NGHE Solution module becomes a 'god module' with scattered task execution logic. Mitigation: Define explicit state machine and interface contracts; enforce module boundaries during implementation.
evidence_needed: Evidence E1: Productivity_target value and units (m³/h or ton/h) from customer.
Evidence E2: Worksite safety plan and operational policies from Safety Manager.
Evidence E3: Network Link SLA (latency, availability, bandwidth) from IT/Network team.
Evidence E4: Vehicle control loop performance specs (cycle time repeatability, accuracy) from Vehicle engineering team.
Evidence E5: Operator skill level and training requirements from Site Manager and Operator.
Evidence E6: Evaluation period definition (duration, worksite conditions, task mix) from Project Manager.
Evidence E7: Telemetry schema and KPI calculation method from NGHE Solution architect.
Evidence E8: Safety constraint list and enforcement logic from Safety Services team.
